questions.text: Which part of a plant carries water from roots to leaves?
questions.explanation: The stem carries water and food between roots and leaves.

questions.text: Plants give out which gas that we breathe in?
questions.explanation: Plants release oxygen, which we need to breathe.

questions.text: Plants prepare their food by a process called ____.
questions.explanation: Plants make food by photosynthesis using sunlight.

questions.text: We get cotton from a plant.
questions.explanation: Cotton comes from the cotton plant. True.

questions.text: Which animal is a herbivore (eats only plants)?
questions.explanation: A cow eats only plants, so it is a herbivore.

questions.text: Which animal eats only meat (carnivore)?
questions.explanation: A lion eats meat, so it is a carnivore.

questions.text: Animals that eat both plants and meat are called ____.
questions.explanation: Omnivores eat both plants and animals (e.g., humans, crows).

questions.text: A snake has no legs.
questions.explanation: A snake has no legs; it slithers. True.

questions.text: Which organ pumps blood in our body?
questions.explanation: The heart pumps blood throughout the body.

questions.text: We breathe with the help of our ____.
questions.explanation: We breathe using our lungs.

questions.text: The hard parts inside our body that give it shape are called ____.
questions.explanation: Bones make our skeleton and give the body shape.

questions.text: We should eat a balanced diet to stay healthy.
questions.explanation: A balanced diet keeps us healthy. True.

questions.text: Which food is a good source of protein?
questions.explanation: Eggs and dal (pulses) are rich in protein.

questions.text: Foods like rice, roti and potato give us ____.
questions.explanation: Carbohydrate foods like rice and roti give energy.

questions.text: Fruits and vegetables give us vitamins.
questions.explanation: Fruits and vegetables are rich in vitamins. True.

questions.text: Which of these is a way to save water?
questions.explanation: Fixing leaking taps saves a lot of water.

questions.text: Water that falls from clouds is called ____.
questions.explanation: Water falling from clouds is rain.

questions.text: We should drink clean, boiled or filtered water.
questions.explanation: Clean water keeps us safe from diseases. True.

questions.text: The air around the Earth is called the ____.
questions.explanation: The layer of air around the Earth is the atmosphere.

questions.text: We need the gas ____ from air to breathe.
questions.explanation: We breathe in oxygen from the air.

questions.text: Polluted air is harmful for our health.
questions.explanation: Polluted air can make us sick. True.

questions.text: Which of these causes pollution?
questions.explanation: Smoke from vehicles pollutes the air.

questions.text: Reduce, reuse and ____ helps protect the environment.
questions.explanation: Reduce, reuse, recycle — the 3 R's protect the environment.

questions.text: Cutting down too many trees is good for nature.
questions.explanation: Cutting too many trees harms nature. False.

questions.text: Which transport is the fastest for long distances?
questions.explanation: Aeroplanes are the fastest for long distances.

questions.text: Long ago, people used ____ carts pulled by bullocks.
questions.explanation: Bullock carts were used for transport long ago.

questions.text: We should follow traffic rules to stay safe.
questions.explanation: Following traffic rules keeps us safe. True.

questions.text: Which of these is a good habit?
questions.explanation: Helping elders is a good habit.

questions.text: ____ is a harvest festival celebrated in many parts of India.
questions.explanation: Pongal and Baisakhi are harvest festivals.

questions.text: Trees give us wood, fruits, and shade.
questions.explanation: Trees give wood, fruits, shade and oxygen. True.

questions.text: How many teeth help us chew called molars are at the back? Which work is done by teeth?
questions.explanation: Teeth help us chew (bite and grind) our food.
